upstream: Ultralytics YOLOE (YOLOE-11s-seg), open-vocabulary; frozen to a fixed COCO-80 vocabulary at export
licence_note: TIER B, RECIPE-ONLY. AGPL-3.0 is copyleft: a compiled .hbm is a derivative of the Ultralytics weights. This repo ships the RECIPE, NOT any .hbm binary. Redistributing the .hbm carries AGPL obligations; commercial use requires an Ultralytics Enterprise licence. Do not commit or publish the compiled models.
normalisation: data_scale, scale 1/255 (0.003921568627451), mean 0. Baked by the compiler, not by the ONNX graph.

vocabulary.order: COCO-80, upstream order (see COCO80 in export_det.py / export_seg.py)
vocabulary.note: The vocabulary is FROZEN at export: get_text_pe(COCO80) + set_classes + head.fuse() fold the CLIP text embeddings into cv3, so the static .hbm is a fixed-80-class detector. Runtime open-vocab prompting is gone by construction; to change classes, re-run export with a new list.

toolchain.note: opset 19 exports at ONNX IR10; export_*.py caps ir_version to 9 (HBDK 4.x rejects IR > 9). IR9 covers up to opset 20, so this is a header change only.

det.outputs: 6 = 3 scales x [cls, box], NHWC, raw (no DFL/anchors/NMS on the BPU)
det.decode: bcdl YoloLtrbDetector: DFL integral, anchor grid + stride, dist2bbox, class sigmoid, NMS -- all on the CPU. box channels 64 = 4 * reg_max, auto-detected.

seg.outputs: 10 = 3 scales x [cls, box, mask_coef] + proto, NHWC, raw
seg.decode: bcdl: detection decode as above, then per-detection mask = sigmoid(coef @ proto) cropped to the box -- on the CPU after NMS, so the full 32x160x160 stack is never materialised on the BPU.

cosine.note: Standard gates. Not yet measured for this model -- see measured.note. For a split detection head, per-output cosine on the raw cls/box tensors is the layer-A signal; whether it costs box recall / mask IoU is a board question, not a cosine one.

task_metric.name: COCO-80 mAP (det: box mAP; seg: mask mAP), fixed-vocabulary
task_metric.note: No task-metric measurement was salvaged. Declared here as the correct acceptance for a detector/segmenter; measure box mAP and mask mAP on a COCO val subset against the float ONNX (and, ideally, against ground truth) before accepting a quantised build.

measured.note: NOT YET MEASURED IN THIS REPO. Only the two ONNX export scripts survived salvage -- there was no config.yaml, no calibration set, no compile log, and no board numbers. Everything below is null pending a re-run. Do not fabricate: the .hbm size, quantisation split, cosine, latency and mAP are all unknown until the recipe is re-run.

board_verification.note: No board close-out. Recipe reconstructed from the salvaged export scripts; calibration, config and compile were rebuilt to be consistent with the nv12/640 input and the split-head outputs, not verified end to end.

provenance.from_salvage: export_det.py and export_seg.py (the head-surgery logic: freeze vocab via fuse(), split head keeping raw cls/box[/coef/proto] convs, NHWC).
provenance.reconstructed: calib.py (letterbox 640 + pad 114, RGB, route through calib_pack.pack), config_det.yaml / config_seg.yaml (nv12 rt, rgb train, data_scale 1/255, nash-m), compile.sh (las2 pattern with --config), this expected.json.
assumptions: Normalisation is scale-only (1/255, mean 0): the Ultralytics export does not bake /255, so the graph expects RGB in [0,1]. Verify against node_info if a build looks wrong.
Letterbox with pad 114 matches bcdl's YOLO preprocessing; confirm against the deployment code before trusting calibration.
input_name 'images' is the Ultralytics default export input name; confirm from the exported ONNX and adjust config if it differs.
jobs=32 and O2/latency copied from the repo default; not tuned for this model.
